text:
There was a wise old goose once who thought very much about things. One day as autumn came late, he flew down from his lofty perch high atop an oak tree to visit the townsfolk.

The townsfolk were amazed. They had never had such a thoughtful creature visit them. They listened attentively as old Goose goose-talk.

The old goose told them stories from his youth, of adventures with the wind and stars.

Then he turned thoughtful. He thought about the young owls that swooped silently through the dusk.

"I think," said the wise old goose solemnly, "that every creature wants the world to see them young"?

And that is why you must learn things?

Even old owl?

Even the hawks?

Even you, I?<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
